name: native_runtime_v2_openopc_style
description: OpenOPC style benchmark suite for evaluating OpenOPC Native Runtime V2 across long-running coding, resume, permission, subagent, and company-mode workflows.

scenarios.name: Long Session Coding
scenarios.goal: Sustain a 100-turn coding session with automatic compaction while preserving correctness.

scenarios.name: Repo Search And Implement
scenarios.goal: Search a repository, implement a change, and validate it with tools in one continuous runtime session.

scenarios.name: Browser Verification
scenarios.goal: Use browser tools to verify the implementation and report validation status without leaving runtime_v2.

scenarios.name: Permission Intercept
scenarios.goal: Fail closed on dangerous shell or file operations and resume correctly after user approval.

scenarios.name: Subagent Parallel
scenarios.goal: Spawn multiple specialist subagents, coordinate them, and merge results back into the parent runtime.

scenarios.name: Compaction Continue
scenarios.goal: Continue execution after durable compaction with session memory reinjected into the prompt.

scenarios.name: Checkpoint Resume
scenarios.goal: Persist a structured checkpoint, restore transcript state, and resume tool execution without legacy recovery text.

scenarios.name: Company Work Item Execution
scenarios.goal: Execute a company-mode workflow stage with native runtime_v2 semantics and keep progress visible to channels and Office UI.
